content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 867 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

March 7, 1979

Mr. Matt Brodie
Modesto Board of Realtors
422 McHenry Avenue
Modesto, California 95354

Dear Mr. Brodie:

This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation
of February 20, 1979.

You stated that the Modesto Board of Realtors does
not make direct political contributions to candidates
or committees but does so through an established state
political action committee. You also stated that there
was a local ballot measure that the Modesto Board of
Realtors was very interested in and wanted to become
involved by making a political contribution of approximately
$500 to $1,00 direct to the ballot measure committee without
going through the state PAC. The monies for this contribution would be allocated from the Board's general fund.
You informed me that the Modesto Board of Realtors
received its money from voluntary contributions from its
membership and that once a year the members are requested
to send their political contributions direct to the state
PAC. You asked what reporting obligation the Modesto
Board of Realtors would incur if they made such a
contribution to the local ballot measure?

At the suggestion of Regina Kutchins in our Sacramento
office, I spoke with Barbara Campbell, an attorney in our
Legal department, and I relayed the following information
to you concerning your question. Our office advised that
the Modesto Board of Realtors may not have a filing
obligation until $5,000 of political contributions are
made in this calendar year. Government Code Section 82013.
However, Barbara Campbell did strongly urge that you
speak with the state PAC's attorney regarding rules,
regulations and possible restrictions of the Modesto
Board of Realtors to become directly involved with a
political campaign.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322 5660 · EXECUTIVE LEGAL 322 5901 · ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322 6641

Mr. Matt Brodie                                          Page Two
March 7, 1979

     If you should have any questions concerning this
matter, you may contact Barbara Campbell at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Helen P. Andino
                                        Special Compliance
                                        Representative

HPA:jg

sections.question: You asked what reporting obligation the Modesto Board of Realtors would incur if they made such a contribution to the local ballot measure?
sections.conclusion: Our office advised that the Modesto Board of Realtors may not have a filing obligation until $5,000 of political contributions are made in this calendar year. Government Code Section 82013. However, Barbara Campbell did strongly urge that you speak with the state PAC's attorney regarding rules, regulations and possible restrictions of the Modesto Board of Realtors to become directly involved with a political campaign.
sections.question_synthetic: What reporting obligations does the Modesto Board of Realtors have if it makes a direct contribution of $500-$1,000 to a local ballot measure committee from its general fund?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Modesto Board of Realtors may not have a filing obligation under Government Code Section 82013 until $5,000 in political contributions are made in the calendar year. However, FPPC advised consultation with the state PAC's attorney regarding any restrictions on direct campaign involvement.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers, but question and conclusion content are clearly present in letter format. Document confirms telephone conversation of February 20, 1979. Contains Government Code citation (82013) referenced in response.

Government Code Section 82013. However, Barbara Campbell did strongly urge that you speak with the state PAC's attorney regarding rules, regulations and possible restrictions of the Modesto Board of Realtors to become directly involved with a political campaign.

embedding.summary: FPPC Special Compliance Representative Helen P. Andino responds to the Modesto Board of Realtors' inquiry about reporting obligations for making a direct contribution to a local ballot measure committee, providing guidance on the $5,000 threshold for filing obligations.
